report_name: VECT 2.0 Ransomware Flaw Means Paying the Ransom is Pointless—Large Files are Wiped Forever

threat_actors.name: VECT
threat_actors.description: A technically amateurish threat actor group operating a Ransomware-as-a-Service (RaaS) model for their VECT 2.0 malware. The group markets their services on BreachForums and has partnered with TeamPCP.
threat_actors.goals: Financial extortion through data exfiltration, encryption, and extortion.

threat_actors.name: TeamPCP
threat_actors.description: A threat actor group known for supply-chain attacks that has partnered with the VECT group to expand the reach of the VECT 2.0 ransomware.

attack_patterns.name: Data Encrypted for Impact
attack_patterns.description: The VECT 2.0 ransomware's primary function is to encrypt data on victim systems to deny access and extort a ransom.

attack_patterns.name: Inhibit System Recovery
attack_patterns.description: Due to a design flaw, the VECT 2.0 ransomware irreversibly destroys files larger than 128 KB, effectively preventing system and data recovery from the encrypted files.

attack_patterns.name: File and Directory Discovery
attack_patterns.description: The VECT 2.0 ransomware scans the filesystem to find files to target for its encryption and destruction process.

attack_patterns.name: Exfiltration Over Web Service
attack_patterns.description: The VECT 2.0 ransomware is marketed as a triple-threat operation, which includes the capability to exfiltrate victim data prior to encryption.

malware.name: VECT 2.0
malware.description: A ransomware that functions as a destructive wiper for files larger than 128 KB due to a critical flaw in its encryption logic. The malware, which has variants for Windows, Linux, and ESXi, discards the nonces required for decrypting the first three-quarters of large files, making recovery impossible even if the ransom is paid.

identities.name: BreachForums
identities.description: A cybercrime marketplace where the VECT 2.0 ransomware builder was made available.

infrastructure.name: VECT 2.0 C2 Server
infrastructure.description: Command and control infrastructure used by the VECT 2.0 RaaS operation to manage infections and potentially receive exfiltrated data. Nonces for small files are transmitted to the C2, but nonces for large files are not.

indicators.name: VECT 2.0 File Extension
indicators.pattern:
title: VECT 2.0 Ransomware File Extension
id: 9c5b1a3d-8e7f-4b2a-9c1d-0e8f7a6b5c4d
status: experimental
description: Detects the creation of files with the .vect extension, which is used by the VECT 2.0 ransomware/wiper.
references:
  - https://research.checkpoint.com/2026/04/28/vect-ransomware-by-design-wiper-by-accident/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-29
tags:
  - attack.t1486
level: high
logsource:
  category: file_event
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    TargetFilename|endswith: '.vect'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects files created with the `.vect` extension, characteristic of the VECT 2.0 ransomware.

indicators.name: VSSAdmin Shadow Copy Deletion
indicators.pattern:
title: VSSAdmin Shadow Copy Deletion
id: f8a1b2c3-d4e5-4f6a-b7c8-d9e0f1a2b3c4
status: experimental
description: Detects the use of vssadmin.exe to delete volume shadow copies, a common technique used by ransomware to inhibit system recovery.
references:
  - https://research.checkpoint.com/2026/04/28/vect-ransomware-by-design-wiper-by-accident/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-29
tags:
  - attack.t1490
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\vssadmin.exe'
    CommandLine|contains|all:
      - 'delete'
      - 'shadows'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the command `vssadmin.exe delete shadows /all /quiet` used to inhibit system recovery, a common ransomware TTP mentioned in the report's hunting hints.
